summary: Auditor PASS branch. All architectural/module documentation already written by the coder (build_process.md rewritten for planner/compiler split; per-action notes/examples/description.md added under os/system/modules/; XML docs on new tester types Timing/Timings/File slim/Run.Output+Timings). My pass evaluated and applied the 4 proposals filed by codeanalyzer: 2 new OBP Shape Smells in CLAUDE.md + good_to_know.md (producer-raw/consumers-transform; reference+flat-copy), 2 codeanalyzer character updates (Pass 1b dereferenced to CLAUDE.md so the list stays in sync; new Pass 4.5 root-cause-vs-symptom with 14 tells). No documentation gaps remain.

changes.description: Added OBP Shape Smell #5 (producer raw / consumers transform) and #6 (reference + flat copy) — both supported by evidence on this branch.
Mirrored OBP smells #5 and #6 as items 6 and 7 in 'OBP Smell Checklist' with worked examples (File.cs slim, test/run.cs TrimStart pattern).
Pass 1b dereferenced to CLAUDE.md (no more inline 4-item enumeration); inserted Pass 4.5 (root cause vs symptom) between Pass 4 and Pass 5.

claude_md_proposals.reason: OBP Shape Smell #5 (producer-raw/consumers-transform) — three branch-local examples (.Path.TrimStart('/') in test/run.cs + duplicated transforms in report.cs), structural rule that applies across the codebase, well-scoped change.

OBP Shape Smell #6 (reference + flat copy) — File.cs slim on this branch is the worked example; mechanical heuristic (3+ scalar fields reachable through a reference field); tester suite stayed green through the collapse, proving the smell is both detectable and fixable.

character_proposals.reason: Pass 1b dereference — critical follow-up: with CLAUDE.md growing from 4 to 6 items, an inlined Pass 1b would silently miss the new smells. Dereferenced version preserves all Pass 1b behavior (yes/no per item, missing-type naming, call-site collapse list) — only the enumeration moves out.

Pass 4.5 root-cause-vs-symptom — 14-tell checklist sharpens existing technique without scope expansion; mechanical trigger (commit verbs fix/handle/guard against/work around); structured report shape; explicit contrast section ('what a root-cause fix looks like'). No overlap with Pass 1-5.
